A 1200 YARD WIDE SWATH OF DAMAGING THUNDERSTORM WINDS BEGAN 2.75 MILES NORTHWEST OF WALNUT ON THE POTTAWATAMIE-SHELBY COUNTY LINE. WINDS ESTIMATED TO BE AS HIGH AS 90 MPH, SEVERE DAMAGED OR DESTROYED SHEDS AND BARNS. SOME LARGE TREES WERE SNAPPED. THE WIND DAMAGE ENDED APPROXIMATELY 9 MILES SOUTHEAST OF HARLAN. THE DURATION OF THE TSTM WND DMG EVENT WAS 5 MINUTES.

TOTAL OF 9 SEMIS OVERTURNED ALONG I-29. 2 DRIVERS WERE INJURED AND TAKEN BY AMBULANCE. INTERSTATE 29 WAS CLOSED BOTH NORTH AND SOUTHBOUND. DELAYED REPORT. EMERGENCY MANAGEMENT REPORTED THAT FLASH FLOODING BEGAN ON SUNDAY EVENING AND CONTINUED INTO THE EARLY MORNING HOURS WITH NUMEROUS ROADS CLOSED ACROSS CUMING COUNTY BY SUNRISE ON MONDAY. THE STARTING TIME OF THE FLASH FLOODING IS ESTIMATED.

DELAYED REPORT FROM MONDAY. CREEKS FLOODED INTO CORNFIELDS BY LATE AFTERNOON WITH CONTINUED SIGNIFICANT RISES INTO MONDAY EVENING TO LEVELS NOT SEEN BY THIS PERSON SINCE THE EARLY 1980S. MEASURED MORE THAN 5 INCHES OF RAIN ON MONDAY. TIME OF PEAK FLASH FLOODING ESTIMATED.

COLFAX COUNTY EMERGENCY MANAGEMENT REPORTS THAT FLASH FLOODING OCCURRED THROUGH THE EVENING OF JUNE 26 DUE TO A LEVEE FAILURE ON HUGHES PAYZANT DITCH. THIS FLOODING IMPACTED COUNTY ROADS. THE EXTENT OF ROAD DAMGE IS UNKNOWN AND WATERS ARE RECEDING BUT FLOODING CONTINUES ON THE MORNING OF JUNE 27.
